title: Chelonanthus longistylus
intro: Chelonanthus longistylus is a flowering plant species commonly known as the 'Long-styled Chelonanthus' or 'Brazilian Chelonanthus', belonging to the Gentianaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects. Bioactive constituents of Chelonanthus longistylus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas and infusions, as well as being incorporated into capsules and tinctures, making it a popular herbal remedy in some regions.

The order Lamiales is part of the class Magnoliopsida, a subgroup of flowering plants. The class Magnoliopsida is part of the division Magnoliophyta, which includes all flowering plants. Chelonanthus longistylus is also part of the kingdom Plantae.
